reproduced: rocprofv3 --kernel-trace on a tinygrad matmul -> 0 CSV files (exit 0, no kernels)
root_cause: tinygrad's AMDComputeQueue/AMDComputeAQLQueue (tinygrad/runtime/ops_amd.py:54,436) write PM4/AQL packets DIRECTLY into a hardware compute ring (cq.ring[...]) and ring the doorbell themselves (cq.signal_doorbell, lines 434,478) via the KFD/amdgpu ioctl path. It NEVER calls hsa_queue_create or submits through the HSA runtime queue.
why_rocprof_blind: rocprof/rocprofv3 intercept dispatches via the HSA runtime: hooking hsa_queue_create + AQL packet/signal interception to inject PMC programming + timestamps. tinygrad bypasses the entire HSA queue path (direct ring + doorbell), so the rocprof HSA interceptor never sees tinygrad's dispatches -> no trace, no counters. This is INHERENT to HCQ, not a missing flag.

bounded_counter_paths_already_killed.amd-sqtt-oracle-hcq-diff: KILL_PATCH_NO_BODY — the bounded SQTT register patch did not produce tinygrad body instruction packets; would need a broader ROCprofiler command-service integration project

what_is_missing: live PMU compute counters (VALU busy, cache, LDS conflicts, occupancy) for HCQ kernels — needs a deep native-profiled-HCQ runtime project (tinygrad programs PMC in its AQL packets) OR a ROCprofiler command-service integration; both LARGE, both previously deferred; AND the counter backend returns 0 on this stack regardless.
